101.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was brought gold coins and he began taking handfuls one by one, then looking to his right as if consulting someone, then giving. A black man with his hair shaved, wearing two white garments, had a mark of prostration between his eyes.
101.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ dibawakan dinar-dinar dan beliau mulai mengambilnya segenggam demi segenggam, lalu menoleh ke kanannya seolah berkonsultasi dengan seseorang, kemudian memberikannya. Seorang lelaki berkulit hitam dengan rambut dicukur, mengenakan dua pakaian putih, memiliki bekas sujud di antara kedua matanya.'

102.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came while the Prophet ﷺ was bowing. The Prophet ﷺ heard the sound of Abu Bakra's sandal as he was hurrying, wanting to catch the rak'a. When the Prophet ﷺ finished, he said: 'Who was the one running?' Abu Bakra said: 'It was me.' He said: 'May Allah increase your eagerness — do not repeat this.'
102.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia datang sementara Nabi ﷺ sedang ruku'. Nabi ﷺ mendengar suara sandal Abu Bakra ketika ia berlari ingin mendapati rakaat. Ketika Nabi ﷺ selesai, beliau bersabda: 'Siapa yang berlari tadi?' Abu Bakra berkata: 'Itu aku.' Beliau bersabda: 'Semoga Allah menambah semangatmu — jangan ulangi ini.'

103.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he witnessed the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on his mule, standing, when a pregnant woman was brought and she said that she had committed fornication. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ told her to go until she had given birth, then to come back.
103.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menyaksikan Rasulullah ﷺ di atas bagalnya, berdiri, ketika seorang wanita hamil dibawa dan ia menyatakan telah berzina. Rasulullah ﷺ menyuruhnya pergi hingga melahirkan, kemudian kembali.'

104.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man from Persia came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'My lord has killed your lord' — meaning Kisra. He said: And it was said to the Prophet ﷺ that Kisra had made his daughter his successor. He said: 'A people will never succeed who are ruled by a woman.'
104.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki dari Persia datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Tuhanku telah membunuh tuhanmu' — yakni Kisra. Ia berkata: Dan dikatakan kepada Nabi ﷺ bahwa Kisra telah mengangkat putrinya sebagai penggantinya. Beliau bersabda: 'Suatu kaum tidak akan pernah berhasil yang diperintah oleh seorang wanita.'

105.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When two Muslims face each other with their swords and one kills the other, both the killer and the killed are in the Fire.' It was said: 'This is the killer — but what about the killed?' He said: 'He intended to kill his companion.'
105.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling berhadapan dengan pedang mereka dan salah satunya membunuh yang lain, maka si pembunuh dan yang terbunuh sama-sama di neraka.' Ditanyakan: 'Ini si pembunuh — tapi mengapa yang terbunuh?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia berniat membunuh temannya.'

106.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The people will be driven over the Sirat on the Day of Resurrection, and the sides of the Sirat will fling them into the Fire like moths falling into a flame. Allah, Blessed and Exalted, will save by His mercy whoever He wills. Then the angels will be given permission to intercede.'
106.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Manusia akan digiring melintasi Shirat pada Hari Kiamat, dan sisi-sisi Shirat akan melemparkan mereka ke dalam api seperti ngengat yang jatuh ke dalam nyala api. Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi akan menyelamatkan siapa yang Dia kehendaki dengan rahmat-Nya. Kemudian para malaikat diberi izin untuk memberi syafaat.'

107.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The terror of al-Masih al-Dajjal will not enter Madinah. It will have seven gates on that day, each gate guarded by two angels.'
107.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketakutan al-Masih al-Dajjal tidak akan masuk ke Madinah. Pada hari itu Madinah memiliki tujuh pintu, setiap pintu dijaga oleh dua malaikat.'

108.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, who is the best of people?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does good deeds.' He said: 'And who is the worst of people?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does evil deeds.'
108.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapakah manusia yang terbaik?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Ia berkata: 'Dan siapakah manusia yang terburuk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'

109.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakra that he said: 'I went with my father to visit Mu'awiya ibn Abi Sufyan, and we were admitted to him. He said: "O Abu Bakra, tell me something you heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ." He said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to delight in righteous dreams and he would ask about them."'
109.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakra bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku pergi bersama ayahku mengunjungi Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, dan kami diizinkan masuk. Ia berkata: "Wahai Abu Bakra, ceritakan padaku sesuatu yang kamu dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ." Ia berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ sangat menyukai mimpi yang baik dan beliau sering bertanya tentangnya."'

110.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: 'Listen — from my nation will emerge people who are severe, sharp, their tongues are smooth with the Quran but it does not pass their collarbones. When you see them, kill them; then if you see them again, kill them.'
110.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ingatlah — dari umatku akan muncul kaum-kaum yang keras, tajam, lisan mereka lancar dengan Al-Qur'an namun tidak melewati tulang selangka mereka. Apabila kalian melihat mereka, bunuhlah mereka; kemudian jika kalian melihat mereka lagi, bunuhlah mereka.'

111.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muslim ibn Abi Bakra that he passed by his father while he was supplicating and saying: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief, poverty, and the punishment of the grave.' He said: 'I took these words from him and used to supplicate with them after every prayer. He passed by me while I was supplicating with them and said: "O my son, where did you learn these words?" I said: "O father, I heard you supplicate with them after every prayer." He said: "Then hold on to them, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to say them."'
111.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muslim ibn Abi Bakra bahwa ia melewati ayahnya yang sedang berdoa dan berkata: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kekufuran, kemiskinan, dan azab kubur.' Ia berkata: 'Aku mengambil kata-kata ini darinya dan biasa berdoa dengannya setelah setiap shalat. Ayahku melewatiku ketika aku sedang berdoa dengannya dan berkata: "Wahai anakku, dari mana kamu belajar kata-kata ini?" Aku berkata: "Wahai ayah, aku mendengarmu berdoa dengannya setelah setiap shalat." Ia berkata: "Maka pegang teguhlah, karena Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengucapkannya."'

112.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was leading people in prayer, and al-Hasan ibn 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with them both) used to climb onto his back when he prostrated. He did this more than once. They said to him: 'By Allah, you do something with this child that we have not seen you do with anyone else.' He said: 'This son of mine is a master, and Allah may reconcile through him two groups of Muslims.'
112.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ sedang mengimami orang-orang shalat, dan al-Hasan ibn 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) biasa naik ke punggungnya ketika beliau sujud. Ia melakukan ini lebih dari sekali. Mereka berkata kepadanya: 'Demi Allah, engkau melakukan sesuatu dengan anak ini yang tidak pernah kami lihat engkau lakukan dengan siapa pun.' Beliau bersabda: 'Putraku ini adalah seorang pemimpin, dan Allah mungkin mendamaikan melaluinya dua kelompok dari kaum Muslim.'

113.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not turn back after me into disbelievers, striking one another's necks.' Ibn Sirin said: 'Into those who have gone astray, striking one another's necks.'
113.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian kembali setelahku menjadi orang-orang kafir yang saling memukul leher satu sama lain.' Ibn Sirin berkata: 'Menjadi orang-orang yang sesat, saling memukul leher satu sama lain.'

114.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was summoned to give testimony. He came to the house and a man stood up from his seat for him. He said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade that when a man stands up from his seat for another man, the latter takes his place — rather sit beside him.'
114.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia dipanggil untuk memberikan kesaksian. Ia datang ke rumah dan seorang lelaki berdiri dari tempat duduknya untuknya. Ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ melarang bahwa ketika seorang lelaki berdiri dari tempat duduknya untuk orang lain, orang itu mengambil tempatnya — melainkan duduklah di sebelahnya.'

115.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A group from my nation will certainly settle in a land called Basra, beside which is a river with many palm trees. They will increase in number there and multiply.'
115.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sungguh sekelompok dari umatku akan menetap di suatu tanah bernama Basrah, di sebelahnya terdapat sungai dengan banyak pohon kurma. Mereka akan bertambah banyak jumlahnya di sana.'

116.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that on that day the Messenger of Allah ﷺ mounted his she-camel and then stopped and said: 'Do you know what day this is?' — and he mentioned the meaning of the hadith of Ibn Abi 'Adi, and in it he said: 'Let those present convey this to those who are absent' — twice — 'for perhaps the one who receives the message understands it better than those who heard it directly.'
116.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa pada hari itu Rasulullah ﷺ menaiki untanya kemudian berhenti dan bersabda: 'Apakah kalian tahu hari apa ini?' — dan ia menyebut makna hadits Ibn Abi 'Adi, dan di dalamnya beliau bersabda: 'Hendaklah yang hadir menyampaikan kepada yang tidak hadir' — dua kali — 'karena mungkin orang yang menerima pesan memahaminya lebih baik dari yang mendengarnya langsung.'

117.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Indeed Allah, Blessed and Exalted, will support this religion through people who have no share in the Hereafter.'
117.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi akan memperkuat agama ini melalui kaum-kaum yang tidak memiliki bagian di akhirat.'

118.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he witnessed the Prophet ﷺ when a messenger came to bring him the good news of a victory of his army over their enemy, and his head was in the lap of 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). He rose and fell in prostration, then he began questioning the messenger and the messenger informed him.
118.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menyaksikan Nabi ﷺ ketika seorang utusan datang membawa kabar gembira kemenangan pasukannya atas musuh mereka, sementara kepalanya berada di pangkuan 'Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha). Beliau bangkit dan bersujud, kemudian mulai menanyai utusan tersebut dan utusan itu memberitahunya.'

119.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever shows off, Allah will expose him; and whoever does things for show, Allah will expose him on the Day of Judgment.'
119.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berbuat agar didengar orang, Allah akan mempermalukannya; dan barangsiapa berbuat agar dilihat orang, Allah akan mempermalukannya di Hari Kiamat.'

120.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was bowing, so he bowed before reaching the row and then walked to the row. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Who was the one who bowed and then walked to the row?' Abu Bakra said: 'It was me.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'May Allah increase your eagerness — but do not repeat this.'
120.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia datang dan Rasulullah ﷺ sedang ruku', maka ia ruku' sebelum mencapai shaf kemudian berjalan ke shaf. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Siapa yang ruku' kemudian berjalan ke shaf?' Abu Bakra berkata: 'Itu aku.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Semoga Allah menambah semangatmu — tetapi jangan ulangi ini.'

121.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he entered the mosque while the Prophet ﷺ was bowing, so he bowed before reaching the row. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'May Allah increase your eagerness — but do not repeat this.'
121.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia masuk masjid sementara Nabi ﷺ sedang ruku', maka ia ruku' sebelum mencapai shaf. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Semoga Allah menambah semangatmu — tetapi jangan ulangi ini.'

122.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ started for the Fajr prayer and gestured to his companions to stay in their places. He went and came back with his head dripping, then led the people in prayer.
122.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memulai shalat Subuh dan memberi isyarat kepada para sahabatnya untuk tetap di tempat mereka. Beliau pergi dan kembali dengan kepala menetes, kemudian mengimami orang-orang shalat.'

123.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw some people praying the Duha prayer and said: 'They are praying a prayer that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not pray, nor did most of his companions (may Allah be pleased with them).'
123.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat orang-orang shalat Dhuha dan berkata: 'Mereka sedang shalat yang tidak pernah Rasulullah ﷺ shalati, dan tidak pula kebanyakan para sahabatnya (radhiyallahu 'anhum).'

124.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Do not turn back after me into disbelievers, striking one another's necks.'
124.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian kembali setelahku menjadi orang-orang kafir yang saling memukul leher satu sama lain.'

125.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man praised another man in the presence of the Prophet ﷺ. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Woe to you! You have cut your companion's neck' — saying it repeatedly. 'If one of you must praise his companion, let him say: I think so-and-so is such-and-such, and Allah is his Reckoner, and I do not commend anyone before Allah.'
125.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki memuji orang lain di hadapan Nabi ﷺ. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Celaka kamu! Kamu telah memotong leher temanmu' — mengulanginya berkali-kali. 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian harus memuji temannya, hendaklah ia berkata: Aku mengira si fulan begini dan begitu, dan Allah adalah penghitungnya, dan aku tidak memuji siapa pun di hadapan Allah.'

126.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the slingshot. A cousin of his took a stone and used it as a slingshot. He said: 'Am I not telling you that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade it, and you are still using it? By Allah, I will never speak to you again as long as I live — or for as long as I remain.'
126.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang ketapel. Seorang sepupunya mengambil batu dan menggunakannya sebagai ketapel. Ia berkata: 'Bukankah aku memberitahumu bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarangnya, dan kamu masih menggunakannya? Demi Allah, aku tidak akan pernah berbicara denganmu seumur hidupku — atau selama aku masih ada.'

127.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the people spoke much about Musaylima the Liar before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said anything about him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then stood among the people and praised Allah, then spoke about Musaylima and said he is a liar from among thirty liars.
127.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa orang-orang banyak berbicara tentang Musailamah al-Kadzdzab sebelum Rasulullah ﷺ mengucapkan sesuatu tentangnya. Rasulullah ﷺ kemudian berdiri di antara manusia dan memuji Allah, lalu berbicara tentang Musailamah dan menyatakan bahwa ia adalah seorang pendusta dari tiga puluh pendusta.'

128.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu 'Uthman that when Ziyad made his claim of lineage, he met Abu Bakra and said: 'What is this that you have done? I heard Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas saying: My ears heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ as he said: "Whoever claims in Islam a father other than his actual father, Paradise is forbidden for him."' Abu Bakra said: 'And I also heard it from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
128.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu 'Utsman bahwa ketika Ziyad mengklaim nasab, ia bertemu Abu Bakra dan berkata: 'Apa yang telah kalian lakukan ini? Aku mendengar Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas berkata: Kedua telingaku mendengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ beliau bersabda: "Barangsiapa dalam Islam mengaku kepada ayah selain ayahnya yang sebenarnya, surga haram baginya."' Abu Bakra berkata: 'Dan aku pun mendengarnya dari Rasulullah ﷺ.'

129.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he ordered his son to write to another son of his who was a judge in Sijistan: 'As for what follows, never judge between two people while you are angry, for I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "No one should judge between two people while he is angry."'
129.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia memerintahkan putranya untuk menulis kepada putra lainnya yang menjadi hakim di Sijistan: 'Amma ba'du, jangan sekali-kali memutuskan perkara antara dua orang ketika engkau sedang marah, karena aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Tidak boleh seorang pun memutuskan perkara antara dua orang ketika ia sedang marah."'

130.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were with the Prophet ﷺ and a man praised another man. The Prophet ﷺ said: "You have broken his back. If one of you must praise his companion, let him say: I think him to be such — and Allah is his Reckoner, and I do not commend anyone before Allah — I think him to be such and such."'
130.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami bersama Nabi ﷺ dan seorang lelaki memuji orang lain. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Kamu telah mematahkan punggungnya. Jika salah seorang dari kalian harus memuji temannya, hendaklah ia berkata: Aku mengiranya begini — dan Allah adalah penghitungnya, dan aku tidak memuji siapa pun di hadapan Allah — aku mengiranya begini dan begitu."'

131.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Indeed the fragrance of Paradise can be detected from a distance of a hundred years' travel. But no slave who kills a person under a treaty will smell it — Allah, Blessed and Exalted, will forbid him Paradise and its fragrance."' Abu Bakra said: 'May Allah deafen my ears if I did not hear it.'
131.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Sesungguhnya wangi surga dapat tercium dari jarak perjalanan seratus tahun. Tetapi tidak ada seorang hamba yang membunuh seorang yang berada dalam perjanjian yang akan menciumnya — Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi akan mengharamkan surga dan wanginya baginya."' Abu Bakra berkata: 'Semoga Allah menulikan telingaku jika aku tidak mendengarnya.'

132.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he entered the mosque while the imam was bowing, so he bowed before reaching the row. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'May Allah increase your eagerness — but do not repeat this.'
132.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia masuk masjid sementara imam sedang ruku', maka ia ruku' sebelum mencapai shaf. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Semoga Allah menambah semangatmu — tetapi jangan ulangi ini.'

133.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When two Muslims face each other with their swords and one kills the other, both the killer and the killed are in the Fire.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this is the killer — but what about the killed?' He said: 'He was intending to kill his companion.'
133.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling berhadapan dengan pedang mereka dan salah satunya membunuh yang lain, maka si pembunuh dan yang terbunuh sama-sama di neraka.' Mereka berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, ini si pembunuh — tapi mengapa yang terbunuh?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia berniat membunuh temannya.'

134.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ was narrating to them one day with al-Hasan ibn 'Ali in his lap. He would turn to his companions and narrate to them, then turn to al-Hasan and kiss him. Then he said: 'This son of mine is a master. If he lives, Allah may reconcile through him two groups of Muslims.'
134.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ sedang bercerita kepada mereka suatu hari sementara al-Hasan ibn 'Ali berada di pangkuannya. Beliau berpaling kepada para sahabatnya dan bercerita kepada mereka, kemudian berpaling kepada al-Hasan dan menciumnya. Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Putraku ini adalah seorang pemimpin. Jika ia hidup, Allah mungkin mendamaikan melaluinya dua kelompok dari kaum Muslim.'

135.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "A people who entrust their affairs to a woman will never succeed."'
135.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Suatu kaum yang menyerahkan urusan mereka kepada seorang wanita tidak akan pernah berhasil."'

136.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The terror of al-Masih al-Dajjal will not enter Madinah. It will have seven gates on that day, at each gate two angels.'
136.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketakutan al-Masih al-Dajjal tidak akan masuk ke Madinah. Pada hari itu Madinah memiliki tujuh pintu, di setiap pintu terdapat dua malaikat.'

137.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'A people who entrust their affairs to a woman will not succeed.'
137.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu kaum yang menyerahkan urusan mereka kepada seorang wanita tidak akan berhasil.'

138.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A people who are ruled by a woman will not succeed.'
138.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu kaum yang diperintah oleh seorang wanita tidak akan berhasil.'

139.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said something regarding the sanctity of the day, month, and city, then said: 'Your blood and your wealth are sacred among you, as sacred as this day of yours in this month of yours in this city of yours.'
139.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda sesuatu tentang kesucian hari, bulan, dan kota, kemudian bersabda: 'Darah dan harta kalian adalah suci di antara kalian, seperti sucinya hari ini dalam bulan ini di kota ini.'

140.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked: 'Which of people is the best?' — or 'Which is the most virtuous?' Yazid doubted. He said: 'The one who lives long and does good deeds.' He was asked: 'And which of people is the worst?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does evil deeds.'
140.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ditanya: 'Siapakah manusia yang terbaik?' — atau 'Siapakah yang paling utama?' Yazid ragu. Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Ditanyakan: 'Dan siapakah manusia yang terburuk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'

141.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, which of people is the best?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does good deeds.' He was asked: 'And which of people is the worst?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does evil deeds.'
141.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapakah manusia yang terbaik?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Ditanyakan: 'Dan siapakah manusia yang terburuk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'

142.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delayed the 'Isha prayer for seven nights — Abu Dawud said: eight nights — until a third of the night. Abu Bakr said: 'O Messenger of Allah, if you could bring it forward, it would be better for our night prayer.' So he brought it forward after that.
142.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengakhirkan shalat 'Isya selama tujuh malam — Abu Dawud berkata: delapan malam — hingga sepertiga malam. Abu Bakar berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, jika engkau bisa mempercepatnya, itu lebih baik untuk shalat malam kami.' Maka beliau mempercepat setelah itu.'

143.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man praised a companion in the presence of the Prophet ﷺ. He said: 'Woe to you! You have cut his neck. If you must praise, then say: I think him to be such and such, and Allah is his Reckoner, and I do not commend anyone before Allah, the Most High.'
143.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki memuji seorang teman di hadapan Nabi ﷺ. Beliau bersabda: 'Celaka kamu! Kamu telah memotong lehernya. Jika kamu harus memuji, katakanlah: Aku mengiranya begini dan begitu, dan Allah adalah penghitungnya, dan aku tidak memuji siapa pun di hadapan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi.'

144.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Two months do not diminish — in each of them is an Eid: Ramadan and Dhu al-Hijja.'
144.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Dua bulan tidak berkurang — pada masing-masingnya terdapat Eid: Ramadan dan Dzulhijjah.'

145.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he entered upon some people to testify in a case, and a man stood up from his seat for him. Abu Bakra said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "A man must not make another man stand from his seat and then sit in it — rather sit beside him."'
145.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia masuk kepada orang-orang untuk memberikan kesaksian dalam suatu perkara, dan seorang lelaki berdiri dari tempat duduknya untuknya. Abu Bakra berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Seorang lelaki tidak boleh membuat lelaki lain berdiri dari tempat duduknya lalu duduk di tempatnya — melainkan duduklah di sebelahnya."'

146.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Aslam, Ghifar, Muzayna, and Juhayna are better than Banu Tamim and Banu 'Amir.'
146.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Aslam, Ghifar, Muzaynah, dan Juhaynah lebih baik dari Bani Tamim dan Bani 'Amir.'

147.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I stood in prayer throughout all of Ramadan.' Allah, Blessed and Exalted, knows best — whether he feared that his nation would praise themselves, or because there must inevitably be one who sleeps or is heedless.
147.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku shalat malam sepanjang Ramadan semuanya.' Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi lebih mengetahui — apakah beliau khawatir umatnya akan memuji diri sendiri, atau karena pasti ada yang tidur atau lalai.'

148.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I stood in prayer throughout all of Ramadan.' Qatada said: Allah, Blessed and Exalted, knows best — whether he feared self-praise for his nation. 'Affan said: or he said: 'There must inevitably be one who sleeps or is heedless.'
148.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku shalat malam sepanjang Ramadan semuanya.' Qatadah berkata: Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi lebih mengetahui — apakah beliau khawatir akan pujian diri bagi umatnya. 'Affan berkata: atau beliau berkata: 'Pasti ada yang tidur atau lalai.'

149.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Indeed there will be tribulations, then more tribulations. Listen — one who walks in them is better than one who runs toward them. One who sits in them is better than one who stands in them. One who lies down in them is better than one who sits. When they befall, whoever has sheep, let him go to his sheep.'
149.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya akan ada fitnah-fitnah, kemudian akan ada lebih banyak fitnah. Ingatlah — orang yang berjalan di dalamnya lebih baik dari yang berlari menuju fitnah. Yang duduk di dalamnya lebih baik dari yang berdiri. Yang berbaring di dalamnya lebih baik dari yang duduk. Apabila fitnah itu datang, barangsiapa memiliki kambing, pergilah ke kambingnya.'

150.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that it was said: 'O Messenger of Allah, which of people is the best?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does good deeds.' It was said: 'O Messenger of Allah, which of people is the worst?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does evil deeds.'
150.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ditanyakan: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapakah manusia yang terbaik?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Ditanyakan: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapakah manusia yang terburuk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'
